location.office_time_required: hybrid — combines time in office and time working from home; specific ratio not stated

seniority.level: Senior IC / Staff (experienced program manager leading complex OEM programs)
level_signals: Described as 'Extensive program management experience in complex automotive software development'
Responsible for leading cross-functional teams and full-scale production programs with OEM customers
Provides first-level customer support and owns risk/change management

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Extensive program management experience in complex automotive software development.
Proficient in utilising program management tools (e.g., Jira) to optimise workflow efficiency and ensure seamless project execution.
Robust technical expertise with comprehensive knowledge of the automotive industry.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively across multidisciplinary teams.
Fluent Japanese and business-level English are required for collaboration with international teams and stakeholders

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Knowledge of current AI advancements, including Generative AI, AV 2.0, and Large Language Models (LLMs).
A Master's degree in Engineering or Computer Science.

scope.surface: OEM customer programs — from early-stage advanced development to full-scale production programs for automated/assisted driving AI software
scope.business_model: B2B, automotive OEM partnerships (AI software / foundation models for automakers)
core_responsibilities: Lead and support cross-functional teams in executing complex customer programs, ensuring the successful achievement of program objectives.
Establish and maintain a comprehensive project management framework, including project planning, risk management, change management, and task coordination.
Engage with customers to understand and address their technical requirements and needs.
Collaborate closely with teams across Product, R&D, and other departments to translate customer demands into clear, actionable technical requirements.
Ensure efficient reporting both externally to customers and internally within the organisation.
Work closely with internal development teams to align customer requirements with internal milestone planning and execution.
Provide first-level support to customers, ensuring their needs are met promptly and effectively.

green_flags: Clear and specific language requirements stated (fluent Japanese + business English) — signals genuine customer-facing Japan-market role
Role is described as full-time with hybrid flexibility in Yokohama or Tokyo
Explicit encouragement to apply even if not meeting all requirements
Strong product focus (Embodied AI, foundation models) with real-world customer impact
Inclusive hiring commitments and DEI monitoring process disclosed
red_flags: No compensation or salary range disclosed
No specific years of experience defined despite requiring 'extensive' program management experience — ambiguous seniority bar
Visa sponsorship and work authorisation not addressed anywhere in the JD
Office location depends on 'candidate's place of residence' — lack of clarity on what that means for relocation or remote eligibility

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'extensive' experience language and scope of OEM program leadership)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description — B2B automaker partnerships)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.source set to 'unknown' — no compensation data anywhere in JD
ambiguities: Location: JD says 'office-based role located in Yokohama or Tokyo, depending on the candidate's place of residence' then also references a hybrid working policy — unclear how much office time is expected vs. remote
Years of experience: 'Extensive program management experience' used without quantification — no minimum year count given
Work authorisation and visa sponsorship: not mentioned anywhere in the JD; left as 'unclear'
